About

The person behind the presentation.

Mr. Orville X. Davis
SUNY BEOC Lecturer

I'm Mr. Orville X. Davis, a lecturer at the SUNY Brooklyn Educational Opportunity Center (BEOC), where I work at the intersection of technology, education, and workforce development.

My work focuses on helping students, educators, and professionals develop practical AI literacy — not just how to use the tools, but how to think critically alongside them.

Today's session was built around one core belief: AI should make you think better, not less. The frameworks and strategies I've shared are ones I use daily with real students in real classrooms.

What I bring to the room

Role Lecturer — SUNY Brooklyn EOC
Focus AI Literacy, Workforce Readiness, Instructional Technology
Audience Students, Educators, Academic Institutions
Approach Co-Pilot Framework — Human judgment stays in the driver's seat

The Prompt Framework

Use this every time you prompt.

01 ——
Task
What exactly do you want done? Be specific — explain, summarize, critique, draft.
02 ——
Context
Who is this for? What's the background? The machine can't read your mind.
03 ——
Format
What should the output look like? List, paragraph, table, slide bullets?
04 ——
Tone
What's the required voice and reading level? Professional, accessible, academic?

The machine didn't get smarter — your guidance became clearer.
